notes: Timely monsoon rainfall during Asar is crucial for successful transplanting and yield.
Rice cultivation in hill regions is mostly rain-fed and practiced on terraced fields. Cold-tolerant and medium-duration varieties are preferred to avoid early winter stress.
Timely sowing before late Mangsir gives higher yield.
Cold-tolerant varieties preferred in higher altitudes.
Mostly rain-fed and grown on terraced fields.
Maize cultivation in mountain regions is limited to lower valleys with suitable temperature.
Commercial maize farming in Terai is mainly practiced in winter using irrigation. This season provides higher yield and better market value.
